mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for applications that require efficient organization and searching of vast amounts of unstructured data. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes for deployment and the complexity of managing distributed systems.

steps.title: Install the Milvus CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Milvus command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Milvus database.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/releases/download/v2.5.0/milvus-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf milvus-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv milvus-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Milvus CLI
> Extract the downloaded archive
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create milvus-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials milvus-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> Fetch credentials for the cluster
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials milvus-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster

steps.title: Install Milvus using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Milvus on the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --version 2.5.0 --namespace milvus --create-namespace

```

> Add the Milvus Helm repository
> Install Milvus in the 'milvus' namespace

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Milvus installation.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/k8s/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/k8s/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration
> Apply monitoring configuration

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Milvus installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

> Uninstall the Milvus Helm release
> Delete the namespace and orphaned resources

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your data. This step will upgrade Milvus to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp milvus-pod:/data /backup/milvus-data

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --version 2.6.11 --namespace milvus

kubectl get pods -n milvus

```

> Backup data from the Milvus pod
> Upgrade Milvus to version 2.6.11
> Verify that the Milvus pods are running

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Milvus'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Milvus pods with 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or adjust resource requests/limits in the Milvus Helm chart.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm installation may have encountered an issue during deployment.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm release status with 'helm status milvus --namespace milvus' and troubleshoot accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus pod may be crashing due to configuration errors.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n milvus' to identify the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vector indexing algorithms and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old indexing methods in favor of the new ones.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.5, ensure to migrate your indexing configurations to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the Milvus service running with the output of 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' showing all pods in 'Running' status.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials milvus-cluster --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
